# Beudox HR - Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a Single Page Application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns.

### Core Technologies
- **React 18**: Modern React with hooks and concurrent features
- **TypeScript**: Type-safe development
- **Vite**: Fast build tool and development server
- **React Router DOM**: Client-side routing
- **Supabase**: Backend-as-a-Service for authentication, database, and real-time features
- **React Query**: Server state management and caching
- **Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui**: Utility-first styling with pre-built components

## Application Structure

### Routing & Pages
The application uses React Router with protected routes. Key routes include:
- `/` - Root redirect (to dashboard if authenticated, login otherwise)
- `/login` - Authentication page
- `/forgot-password` - Password recovery
- `/dashboard` - Main dashboard
- `/employees` - Employee list
- `/employees/new` - Add new employee
- `/employees/:id` - Employee profile view
- `/employees/:id/edit` - Edit employee

### Layout System
- **AppLayout**: Main application wrapper with sidebar and top bar
- **AppSidebar**: Collapsible navigation sidebar with sections (MAIN, PEOPLE, FINANCE, WORK, SYSTEM)
- **TopBar**: Dynamic page title header

### Component Organization
- **UI Components**: Extensive use of shadcn/ui components (buttons, forms, dialogs, etc.)
- **Custom Components**: BeudoxLogo, NavLink, layout components
- **Pages**: Feature-specific page components

## Key Files & Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap and React root rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing and providers

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout structure
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar with menu sections
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Page header with dynamic titles
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Logo component with variant support
- `src/components/NavLink.tsx`: Enhanced NavLink with active state styling

### Authentication
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication state management
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`: Database type definitions

### Utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/hooks/use-toast.ts`: Toast notification system

## Database & Backend

### Supabase Integration
- **Authentication**: User login, signup, password reset
- **Database**: PostgreSQL database with real-time subscriptions
- **Edge Functions**: Serverless functions (e.g., invite-employee)

### Database Schema
The application uses Supabase migrations located in `supabase/migrations/` for database schema management.

## Development & Build

### Configuration Files
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `eslint.config.js`: ESLint configuration
- `playwright.config.ts`: End-to-end testing configuration

### Testing
- **Unit Tests**: Vitest for component and utility testing
- **E2E Tests**: Playwright for end-to-end testing

## Business Logic

### HR Features Implemented
- Employee management (CRUD operations)
- User authentication with role-based access
- Dashboard overview
- Responsive design with mobile support

### Planned Features (based on navigation)
- Attendance tracking
- Leave management
- Payroll processing
- Finance management
- Project management
- HR policy management

## Data Flow

1. **Authentication**: Users authenticate via Supabase Auth
2. **Data Fetching**: React Query manages API calls to Supabase
3. **State Management**: Local component state with React hooks
4. **Real-time Updates**: Supabase subscriptions for live data

## Styling & Theming

- **Design System**: Custom theme with CSS variables
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first approach with Tailwind breakpoints
- **Icons**: Lucide React icon library
- **Typography**: Custom font families (--ff-display, --ff-body)

## Performance Considerations

- **Code Splitting**: Vite handles automatic code splitting
- **Lazy Loading**: Components can be lazy-loaded as needed
- **Caching**: React Query caches API responses
- **Bundle Optimization**: Tree shaking and minification via Vite

## Security

- **Authentication**: Supabase handles secure authentication
- **Environment Variables**: Sensitive config stored in environment
- **Type Safety**: TypeScript prevents runtime errors
- **Input Validation**: Zod schemas for form validation
